codeReview.summary: Implementation covers all required API endpoints and frontend pages. However, there are blocking bugs: (1) the delete functionality in the frontend references a non-existent API function, (2) moderator role state is never populated causing the moderator UI to never show, and (3) the report button UI has no handler. Also identified race condition in upvote toggle and unnecessary database queries.

issues.description: deleteDiscussion is imported but does not exist in the API client (apps/web/src/lib/api/community.ts). The API client only has deleteDiscussion (camelCase), but the import references deleteDiscussion which is never defined. This will cause a runtime error when trying to delete.

currentUserRole state is set from fetchCurrentUser() response, but fetchCurrentUser() only returns user.sub (user ID), not the user's role. The role is never fetched and currentUserRole remains null. As a result, isModerator is always false and moderator UI elements never display. The delete button for moderators will be hidden.
Report button is rendered but has no onClick handler - it only calls a commented/empty handler. Users cannot report inappropriate content.

Race condition in upvote toggle: The upvote count is read before the operation, then the operation happens, but the count returned is calculated from the stale object. If concurrent requests happen, the count could be incorrect. Should re-fetch after operation or use database-level atomic operations.
increment_view_count calls get_by_id again after the discussion was already fetched. Redundant database query - the discussion object is already available from the caller and could be passed in.
Upvote button in community list page has no actual upvote handler - the onClick stops propagation but does not call any API or update state. Users can click but nothing happens.

sharedStateObservations.observation: Frontend uses camelCase for API function names (getDiscussion, createDiscussion) but backend router uses snake_case URL paths (create_discussion, delete_discussion). The API client handles this mapping correctly, but the frontend components should be consistent with the exported API function names.
sharedStateObservations.evidence: apps/web/src/lib/api/community.ts exports camelCase functions (getDiscussion, createDiscussion, deleteDiscussion) but apps/web/src/app/community/[id]/page.tsx imports deleteDiscussion which doesn't exist - should be delete_discussion with underscore

sharedStateObservations.observation: Worker skill (frontend-worker) procedure includes verifying interactive functionality, but the transcript shows only typecheck/lint were run. The skillFeedback indicates procedure was followed, but the verification commands run were only static checks, not interactive browser verification as the skill requires.
sharedStateObservations.evidence: verification.commandsRun shows only 'npm run typecheck' and 'npm run lint' - no agent-browser verification despite skill procedure requiring it

sharedStateObservations.observation: The fetchCurrentUser function only returns user.sub, not the full user object with role. Any component needing user role info would need to fetch it separately or the auth system needs to expose this data.
sharedStateObservations.evidence: apps/web/src/lib/auth.ts returns only { sub: user.id } based on router transcript skeleton showing currentUserRole never gets populated

summary: The community module implements all required API endpoints and frontend pages with proper validation (title min 10, content min 20, reply min 10 chars). However, three blocking bugs prevent full functionality: (1) delete button references non-existent API function causing runtime error, (2) moderator role is never fetched so moderator UI never displays, (3) report button has no handler. Additionally found race condition in upvote toggle and missing upvote handler in list view. The module would need these fixes before passing review.
